summary: Re-reviewed app core (upload.js, viewer.js, app.js) — unchanged since last review and clean. Infra clean: nginx valid, sensitive paths 403, non-breaking security headers live, 0 failed SSH auth in 24h, no real secrets in git. One new low finding (SEC-002): viewer renders all pages with no page-count cap (PDF-bomb DoS gap). SEC-001 full resource CSP still deferred — browser testing still down (chrome MCP sandbox flags not yet applied to ~/.claude.json).

findings.title: Full resource CSP (script-src/worker-src/img-src) not yet applied
findings.reason: Must be browser-verified before shipping; chrome-devtools tester is still down (SYSTEM CRITICAL — ~/.claude.json still lacks the --chromeArg=--no-sandbox sandbox flags, so Chrome aborts on launch). Full CSP needs script-src 'wasm-unsafe-eval' for pdf.js WASM decoders; an untested CSP risks silently breaking JPEG2000/JBIG2 PDF rendering. frame-ancestors CSP + clickjacking/privacy headers applied in the meantime.
findings.next_action: Apply full CSP from docs/security-guide.md once browser testing is restored, then confirm a real PDF still renders.

findings.title: viewer.js renderAll() has no page-count cap (PDF-bomb / memory-exhaustion gap)
findings.reason: upload.js bounds file size (50 MB) and validates magic bytes, but viewer.js renders every page into its own canvas with no limit. A crafted high-page-count PDF under 50 MB can exhaust memory on the 1.6 GiB box. Client-side only — affects only the uploader's own tab, no server impact. Violates the security-guide 'max 1000 pages' rule.
findings.next_action: Developer: in viewer.js after getDocument().promise, reject when doc.numPages > 1000 (or render lazily). Flagged in docs/security-guide.md. Not implemented by security per review-don't-rewrite + cannot browser-verify while tester is down.

code_review.upload_validation: magic-bytes + MIME + .pdf extension + empty-check + 50MB limit + filename sanitize — OK
code_review.xss_sinks: no innerHTML-with-user-content, no eval/Function/document.write; textContent used for status/labels — OK
code_review.pdfjs_buffer_handling: loadDocument hands pdf.js a private buffer copy; render-token guards overlapping renders — OK
code_review.secrets_in_git: none (only log-narrative mentions of the words secret/password)

tls: none (HTTP-only; certbot deferred until cronloop.techtools.cz DNS cutover)
